T0050.title: Naked Application
T0050.definition: Naked application refers to the practice of applying for a credit card or loan by submitting only a basic form without any supporting documentation.
T0050.description: Applicants deliberately withhold proof of assets, income, or other supplementary materials, exploiting the lenient underwriting thresholds of certain products. Fraud rings commonly use this method to test banks' or platforms' approval loopholes at scale, screening for channels with weak risk controls to pave the way for subsequent large-scale fraud.

references.title: 2024 H1 Credit Fraud Risk Landscape Report
Black Market Big Data: Exposing Malicious Loan Brokers
